question.content: In the figure, circle O is the circumcircle of pentagon ABCDE, and BD is a diameter of circle O. Draw AC, BE, and CE, and suppose angle AEC equals angle ACF. If CE = CB and angle CBE = 60 degrees, find angle BCE. Answer format requirement: <expr>. Strictly forbidden: any extra text beyond this format.

step_1.content: Since CE equals CB, triangle CBE is isosceles with base BE, so the base angles at B and E are equal.
step_1.key_point: Use CE=CB to identify equal base angles in triangle CBE.

step_3.content: The given angle CBE is 60 degrees, so the equal base angle BEC is also 60 degrees.
step_3.key_point: Transfer the known 60-degree base angle to the other base angle.

step_5.content: Then angle BCE equals 180-60-60, so triangle CBE is equilateral and angle BCE is 60 degrees.
step_5.key_point: Finish with the angle sum of triangle CBE.
